data.title: Cboe’s 24 Hour EDGX Plan And What It Means For Investors
data.description:
• Cboe Global Markets plans to introduce 24-hour, 5-days-per-week trading for U.S. equities on its EDGX exchange, pending regulatory review.
  • The move would extend U.S. stock trading to an around-the-clock schedule on weekdays, aligning more closely with global trading hours.
  • The initiative is aimed at giving investors continuous access to U.S. equities and could influence liquidity patterns and competition across trading venues.

Cboe Global Markets (BATS:CBOE) is pursuing this expanded schedule with its share price at $289.95 and a return of 16.9% year to date. Over the past year, the stock has returned 41.5%, and over 5 years it is up 206.1%, which reflects sustained investor interest in the business.

If regulators clear the plan, 24-hour trading on EDGX could change how you think about liquidity, order timing, and risk management around news events. Investors may start reevaluating when they trade U.S. equities, how they use after-hours liquidity, and how extended access affects the role of other venues that currently concentrate overnight activity.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for Cboe Global Markets by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on Cboe Global Markets.

3 things going right for Cboe Global Markets that this headline doesn't cover.

Round the clock trading on EDGX would extend Cboe’s existing role in options and volatility products into a continuous U.S. cash equity venue. For you as an investor, that matters less for headline volume and more for how Cboe positions itself versus Nasdaq and NYSE in serving global participants who want to trade U.S. stocks during Europe or Asia hours. The plan also leans on Cboe’s technology and market structure expertise, since keeping an order book live 24 hours, 5 days per week requires resilient systems, clear rulebooks, and tight coordination with market makers and brokers.

Advertisement

How This Fits Into The Cboe Global Markets Narrative

  • The move supports the idea that Cboe is expanding across derivatives, data, and global spot markets by offering another venue that can serve international flows and extend use of its trading and data infrastructure.
  • Opening a new trading session window could pressure margins if competition on fees versus exchanges such as Nasdaq and Intercontinental Exchange intensifies faster than any benefit from broader participation.
  • The narrative focuses heavily on options, indices, and data, while around the clock U.S. equity trading on EDGX introduces an additional element of market-structure execution risk and operational complexity that is not fully reflected in earlier qualitative commentary.

Knowing what a company is worth starts with understanding its story. Check out one of the top narratives in the Simply Wall St Community for Cboe Global Markets to help decide what it's worth to you.

The Risks and Rewards Investors Should Consider

  • ⚠️ Additional trading hours could increase technology, staffing, and regulatory compliance costs, and any outage or pricing issue overnight could damage trust in Cboe’s platforms.
  • ⚠️ If liquidity during overnight sessions remains thin or fragmented across rivals such as Nasdaq and NYSE affiliated venues, spreads could stay wide and limit the practical value of 24 hour access for many investors.
  • 🎁 If EDGX gains a meaningful share of overnight U.S. equity activity, it could deepen Cboe’s relationships with global brokers and institutions that already route flow to its options and derivatives markets.
  • 🎁 A continuous book may increase the relevance of Cboe’s data products, since real time pricing across more hours of the day can feed demand for analytics, indices, and risk tools tied to U.S. equities.

What To Watch Going Forward

From here, focus on whether regulators approve the EDGX proposal, how quickly Cboe can attract market makers and institutional flow into the new sessions, and how spreads and depth look overnight compared with regular hours. It is also worth tracking how competitors respond, such as launching similar overnight offerings or adjusting fee schedules, and whether Cboe highlights any link between extended hours, data usage, and overall trading metrics in future updates.

data.title: Marvell Technology (MRVL) Is Up 22.0% After NVIDIA’s $2 Billion AI Partnership And Investment - Has The Bull Case Changed?
data.description:

Outshine the giants: these 21 early-stage AI stocks could fund your retirement.

Marvell Technology Investment Narrative Recap

To own Marvell today, you need to believe its core AI data center and optics franchises can keep converting design wins into sustainable, profitable growth despite heavy reliance on a few hyperscale customers. The NVIDIA NVLink Fusion partnership and US$2.00 billion preferred investment appear to reinforce the near term AI data center catalyst, but they do not remove key risks around customer concentration, custom XPU project lumpiness, and the ongoing need for very high R&D spending.

The NVIDIA preferred stock deal is the clearest link between this news and Marvell’s existing AI narrative. It introduces the possibility of future dilution if the Series A converts into up to 21,778,000 common shares, which matters for any thesis built on earnings per share growth and buybacks. At the same time, being wired into NVIDIA’s AI factory and AI RAN ecosystems directly supports Marvell’s push to grow its share of the US$94 billion data center TAM through custom silicon and high speed interconnect.

Yet against this stronger AI story, investors should also weigh the growing risk that a few large cloud customers, now driving roughly three quarters of revenue, could one day decide to scale back external silicon purchases...

data.title: Only Two Days Left To Cash In On Zanlakol's (TLV:ZNKL) Dividend
data.description:
Regular readers will know that we love our dividends at Simply Wall St, which is why it's exciting to see Zanlakol Ltd (TLV:ZNKL) is about to trade ex-dividend in the next two days. The ex-dividend date is two business days before a company's record date in most cases, which is the date on which the company determines which shareholders are entitled to receive a dividend. The ex-dividend date is of consequence because whenever a stock is bought or sold, the trade can take two business days or more to settle. Therefore, if you purchase Zanlakol's shares on or after the 9th of April, you won't be eligible to receive the dividend, when it is paid on the 29th of April.

The company's upcoming dividend is ₪1.0046592 a share, following on from the last 12 months, when the company distributed a total of ₪1.43 per share to shareholders. Based on the last year's worth of payments, Zanlakol has a trailing yield of 3.6% on the current stock price of ₪40.08. If you buy this business for its dividend, you should have an idea of whether Zanlakol's dividend is reliable and sustainable. So we need to check whether the dividend payments are covered, and if earnings are growing.

AI is about to change healthcare. These 20 stocks are working on everything from early diagnostics to drug discovery. The best part - they are all under $10bn in marketcap - there is still time to get in early.

Dividends are typically paid from company earnings. If a company pays more in dividends than it earned in profit, then the dividend could be unsustainable. Zanlakol paid out a comfortable 41% of its profit last year. A useful secondary check can be to evaluate whether Zanlakol generated enough free cash flow to afford its dividend. Zanlakol paid out more free cash flow than it generated - 153%, to be precise - last year, which we think is concerningly high. It's hard to consistently pay out more cash than you generate without either borrowing or using company cash, so we'd wonder how the company justifies this payout level.

While Zanlakol's dividends were covered by the company's reported profits, cash is somewhat more important, so it's not great to see that the company didn't generate enough cash to pay its dividend. Were this to happen repeatedly, this would be a risk to Zanlakol's ability to maintain its dividend.

Check out our latest analysis for Zanlakol

Click here to see how much of its profit Zanlakol paid out over the last 12 months.

Advertisement

Have Earnings And Dividends Been Growing?

Businesses with strong growth prospects usually make the best dividend payers, because it's easier to grow dividends when earnings per share are improving. If earnings decline and the company is forced to cut its dividend, investors could watch the value of their investment go up in smoke. Fortunately for readers, Zanlakol's earnings per share have been growing at 19% a year for the past five years. Earnings have been growing at a decent rate, but we're concerned dividend payments consumed most of the company's cash flow over the past year.

Many investors will assess a company's dividend performance by evaluating how much the dividend payments have changed over time. In the past 10 years, Zanlakol has increased its dividend at approximately 11% a year on average. It's great to see earnings per share growing rapidly over several years, and dividends per share growing right along with it.

Final Takeaway

Is Zanlakol worth buying for its dividend? We're glad to see the company has been improving its earnings per share while also paying out a low percentage of income. However, it's not great to see it paying out what we see as an uncomfortably high percentage of its cash flow. Overall, it's hard to get excited about Zanlakol from a dividend perspective.

While it's tempting to invest in Zanlakol for the dividends alone, you should always be mindful of the risks involved. To help with this, we've discovered 1 warning sign for Zanlakol that you should be aware of before investing in their shares.
